spending.headline: Add ₹5,000 to savings monthly
spending.description: Your savings cover 2 months. Aim for 3–6 months to protect against sudden needs.

evidence.explanation: Emergency corpus at ₹8,500 against an ideal of ₹45,000 leaves a significant gap.

spending.headline: Put your idle money to work
spending.description: ₹3,200 is sitting idle beyond your safety buffer. Investing can help grow money faster.

evidence.explanation: Idle funds above emergency needs could earn returns through simple investment vehicles.

spending.headline: Reduce high spending areas
spending.description: A large part of your spend is in food & groceries. Cutting just 10% here can save ₹1,200 yearly.

evidence.explanation: Food & groceries is the highest spend category, making it a good target for optimization.

borrowing.headline: Keep EMI burden under control
borrowing.description: Your EMI takes up 19% of income. Try to keep it below 20% for financial flexibility.

evidence.explanation: EMI burden is near the manageable threshold but could tip over in low-income months.

borrowing.headline: Your credit score needs attention
borrowing.description: At 620, your credit score is below the good range. Timely payments can help improve it steadily.

evidence.explanation: Credit score has been declining and is now in the fair range.

protection.headline: Increase your health coverage
protection.description: Your health cover is at 42% of the recommended level. A top-up plan can bridge this gap affordably.

evidence.explanation: Health coverage at ₹1.5L vs recommended ₹3.6L leaves significant exposure.

protection.headline: Your life cover has a large gap
protection.description: Current life cover is only 13% of what's recommended. Consider a term plan for affordable coverage.

evidence.explanation: Life cover at ₹2L vs recommended ₹15L creates a major protection gap.

wealth.headline: Start investing more regularly
wealth.description: Only 3% of your income goes to investments. Even small SIPs of ₹500 can compound over time.

evidence.explanation: Investment rate is well below the recommended 15-20% range.

wealth.headline: Diversify beyond fixed deposits
wealth.description: 65% of your portfolio is in FDs. Adding equity exposure can improve long-term returns significantly.

evidence.explanation: Heavy FD allocation limits growth potential; equity exposure is only 8%.

tax.headline: Start filing your income tax
tax.description: Tax filing is not done yet. Filing on time avoids penalties and builds your financial record.

evidence.explanation: Tax filing status is 'No', indicating returns have not been filed.

tax.headline: Claim available tax deductions
tax.description: You're not using any tax-saving instruments. EPF, ELSS, or PPF can reduce your tax outgo.

evidence.explanation: Zero tax-saving instruments availed out of 5 possible options.
